name: Donna Brazile
subject: This is the choice
body:
Help us keep fighting
---
**Donald Trump will mark 100 days in office tomorrow -- and we'll celebrate 100 days of resistance. Help us keep fighting by becoming a Founding Donor to our 100 Day Resistance Fund:
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c8bb/4067b0a/2f38a19dd/7e747a55/3461131038/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/100-Day-Resistance-Fund-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170428_db_v2s2&source=em_20170428_db_v2s2&refcode=em_20170428_db_v2s2")****
Friend --

In Barack Obama's first hundred days in office, he signed a stimulus bill to jump start our economic recovery, signed the Lilly Ledbetter Fair Pay Act into law, and expanded children's health insurance -- all passed by a Democratic majority in Congress.

In Donald Trump's first hundred days, he made it harder for families to buy a home and save for retirement, tried to jam through a bill that would take health care away from 24 million Americans, and announced a big tax cut for rich people and corporations at the expense of working people, with his Republican apologists in Congress cheering him on.

So do you want to elect more Democrats like Barack Obama or Republicans like Donald Trump? That's the choice we need to put in front of every single person who goes to the ballot box -- whether it's for special elections in Montana and Georgia, governor's races in Virginia and New Jersey in the fall, or the midterms next year.

We need your help to make sure we can do that. Before the end-of-month deadline on Sunday, chip in $10 or more.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[ QUICK DONATE: $10](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c8bb/4067b0a/2f38a19dd/7e747a53/3461131038/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/100-day-resistance-fund?express_lane=true&amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+QUICK+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20170428_db_v2s2&source=em_20170428_db_v2s2&refcode=em_20170428_db_v2s2")

body:
What we did. |

Friend, Since the President emailed you, we’re now only $102,833 away from hitting our goal before the April end-of-month FEC deadline. Because we only have LESS THAN 3 DAYS until the deadline, a committed group of Trump supporters have stepped up to DOUBLE MATCH ALL CONTRIBUTIONS until 11:59 PM on Sunday. So if you contribute $1 before the FEC deadline, it will have a total effect of $3. CONTRIBUTE $1 = $3 Please note: your contribution MUST be made before the 11:59 PM deadline on Sunday in order to count for the DOUBLE MATCH OPPORTUNITY. Contribute just $1 before the FEC deadline. Thanks, Trump Headquarters ---------- Forwarded message ---------- From: Donald J. Trump Date: April 27, 2017 at 11:24 AM Subject: It was not supposed to happen - To: dpwillis67@gmail.com

Friend, We were “not supposed to win.” The media said that Hillary was supposed to be president. Obama’s legacy was supposed to continue. Our border was supposed to stay wide open. Our economy was supposed to stay rigged. Our massive bureaucratic state was supposed to grow even bigger. But there was one problem...The American people had a different plan for our country. And here in America, We The People always have the final word. You demanded change. You demanded a new direction that puts America First. You wanted to see America win again. And as YOUR president, I’m working every single day to ensure your dreams become our country’s future. But those same people that said we weren’t supposed to win are now trying to destroy everything you and I have built. That’s why we set goals EVERY single month to ensure we have the resources to fight back against any and all attacks. Now I need you to step up and protect what we’ve already achieved and everything we will accomplish over the next three years. Please make a contribution of just $1 before the April end-of-month FEC deadline. CONTRIBUTE $1 Thank you and God bless you, Donald J. Trump President of the United States

name: Scott Arceneaux
subject: Leave no doubt.
body:
#
Team --
Last week, the _Tampa Bay Times_ praised Andrew, saying _" Gillum brought Democrats roaring to their feet in Manatee County with a soaring speech."_
But in that same article, they questioned our campaign's "staying power," that despite our many strengths, our campaign was still "uncertain."
And that's why I'm reaching out today, with just a few days left before our end-of-month fundraising deadline. Because we need to prove to the state and the nation that we have staying power, that this is a momentum and a movement we can sustain through our election and beyond.
We have an opportunity to elect a Democrat as our Governor in Florida for the first time in over 20 years. Not just any Democrat either -- but one with integrity, who stays true to our values no matter what, who I firmly believe can rebuild our state into one that works for all of us, not just those at the top.
**That 's why it's critical that we leave no doubt. And that's why I'm asking now:**
[**Will you contribute to our insurgent campaign today, and help us keep up the momentum we need to win?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb336d/9e00690/482e07d2/24034e16/1849989409/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-april2017?refcode=em20170428-stayingpower-c")
It's not going to be easy. But I promise, it will be worth it.
Bring it home,
Scott Arceneaux
Andrew Gillum for Governor
**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb336d/9e00690/482e07d2/24034e16/1849989409/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-april2017?refcode=em20170428-stayingpower-c")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. clean_body: Team -- Last week, the Tampa Bay Times praised Andrew, saying " Gillum brought Democrats roaring to their feet in Manatee County with a soaring speech." But in that same article, they questioned our campaign's "staying power," that despite our many strengths, our campaign was still "uncertain." And that's why I'm reaching out today, with just a few days left before our end-of-month fundraising deadline. Because we need to prove to the state and the nation that we have staying power, that this is a momentum and a movement we can sustain through our election and beyond. We have an opportunity to elect a Democrat as our Governor in Florida for the first time in over 20 years. Not just any Democrat either -- but one with integrity, who stays true to our values no matter what, who I firmly believe can rebuild our state into one that works for all of us, not just those at the top. That 's why it's critical that we leave no doubt. And that's why I'm asking now: "" It's not going to be easy. But I promise, it will be worth it. Bring it home, Scott Arceneaux Andrew Gillum for Governor "" Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

name: Keith Ellison
subject: You should be a Founding Donor before tomorrow
body:
Help us keep fighting
---
**Donald Trump will mark 100 days in office tomorrow -- and we'll celebrate 100 days of resistance. Help us keep fighting by becoming a Founding Donor to our 100 Day Resistance Fund:
**[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c8b3/4067b0a/2f31981f8/7e746cf5/4063028464/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/100-Day-Resistance-Fund-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170428_ke_nd&source=em_20170428_ke_nd&refcode=em_20170428_ke_nd")****
Friend --

Donald Trump likes to say we're gonna win so much, we'll be tired of all the winning.

Well, he's right about one thing: those of us who've spent these first hundred days of the Trump administration fighting back have done a lot winning.

We won when we stopped a man with a record of mistreating his workers from heading the Department of Labor.

We won when we saved the Affordable Care Act from the chopping block, and kept the GOP from leaving millions of Americans out in the cold.

And we've already started winning elections, from city councils in Alaska and Illinois to state legislatures in Delaware and Connecticut. And we won a big victory last week in Georgia, setting us up for a chance to start replacing Republicans in Congress with Democrats.

We've won so much -- but are we tired of winning? Hell no! If you're like me, you're fired up to keep taking on Trump and the GOP and coming out on top.

**[So here's our next chance to win big: by fully funding our 100 Day Resistance Fund before tomorrow.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c8b3/4067b0a/2f31981f8/7e746cf2/4063028464/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/100-Day-Resistance-Fund-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+So+heres+our+next+chance+to+win+bigCUSTO&utm_campaign=em_20170428_ke_nd&source=em_20170428_ke_nd&refcode=em_20170428_ke_nd")**

Our purpose with this fund is to make sure we're in fighting form to beat Trump's allies at the polls. Hitting our goal will be a massive blow to the GOP's efforts to keep a stranglehold on Congress, governorships, and state legislatures -- which means they won't be able to put the Trump agenda into practice.

If you're ready to keep winning, pitch in $3 or more and become a Founding Donor to the 100 Day Resistance Fund today.

This weekend marks the 100th day in office for Donald Trump.
He has made known anti-Semite and misogynist, Steve Bannon, his right-hand man. He has stripped Americans of their right to Internet privacy. He has tried to undo decades of progress for women's health. And, he has attempted to ram through an un-American travel ban.
It's been a tough 100 days, and we have a long road ahead of us if we're going to hold the line on the progress we've made. **But we're a team, and we're ready to fight.**
**You're one of the strongest advocates for progress we have, so we're asking you directly:[Make your first online contribution now -- just $3 or whatever makes sense for you -- to step up for this team and resist Donald Trump and Steve Bannon's dangerous agenda.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd4f/2a380d31/1576c0f9/fef9f2/322032304/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX2dlbmVyYWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDQyOV9GaXJzdFRpbWVfbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_general?refcode=e20170429_FirstTime_nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")**
It can be easy to lose sight of, but thanks to you, there have been some huge bright spots.
Trump's attempt to repeal the Affordable Care Act failed. Now, millions of Americans get to keep the live-saving coverage they depend on.
We very nearly prevented an unfit nominee, Betsy DeVos, from becoming Secretary of Education.
And thanks to you, we are well positioned for the fights ahead. Now, we just need the resources to hold our ground.
[**Please, make your first online gift of just $3 or more today.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd4f/2a380d31/1576c0f9/fef9f2/322032304/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX2dlbmVyYWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDQyOV9GaXJzdFRpbWVfbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_general?refcode=e20170429_FirstTime_nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")

name: Sen. Chris Van Hollen (via DSCC.org)
subject: re: Trump's tax plan (add your name)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Send a message to Donald Trump about his outrageous tax plan. Will you stand with us in this fight?
---
[****](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e013/2b07114c/5c80f2c7/736de96b/651542430/VEsH/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/no-breaks?source=DPE-20170428-VanHollen-ProsHV")
Team, here's my message to Donald Trump:

> _We 're not going to add $7 trillion to our deficit so you can hand out enormous tax windfalls to your billionaire friends. Full Stop._
Look, before I joined the Senate, I spent 6 years standing up to Paul Ryan's budget plans in the House -- I know the games Republicans play to claim their so-called 'reforms' will make our economy grow. The reality is that good tax reform must lay out a plan to help the middle class, not just millionaires and billionaires.

And I know bad tax reform from the Bush years. Giant corporations and the super wealthy lined their pockets with millions, heck, even billions of dollars. Our economy? They ran it into the ground.

So I'm not buying this latest glossy "trickle down" tax cut idea, because we already know who it fails and who it helps instead. It will benefit the wealthiest in our country -- people like Donald Trump -- at the expense of everyone else.

There's absolutely no justification for rewriting our tax laws so the super wealthy can get bigger breaks -- and here's what adds insult to injury: there's no guarantee that the middle class won't pay even more under Donald Trump's plan.

Democrats are going to fight this tooth and nail -- because we won't stand by and let everyday people foot the bill so millionaires like Donald Trump get richer. And we won't let them burden our country and future generations with debt while they enrich themselves.

It's an outrage. And we must stop it. We're counting on our grassroots supporters to add their voices with Senate Democrats in this fight.

**Will you add your name to say NO to Donald Trump 's tax plan?:**

[**http://dscc.org/no-breaks-for-billionaires**](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e013/2b07114c/5c80f2c7/736de96b/651542430/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/no-breaks?source=DPE-20170428-VanHollen-ProsHV")

Thank you for keeping up the fight,

Senator Chris Van Hollen

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e013/2b07114c/5c80f2c7/736de96a/651542430/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e013/2b07114c/5c80f2c7/736de965/651542430/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. | | **" Indiana's Senate race is expected to be one of the top targets for Republicans because Donnelly is the only Democrat elected statewide in Indiana, which voted for President Trump by nearly 20 percentage points." -USA Today, 4/10/17** Peter,

We all know that Indiana is at the top of Mitch McConnell and Donald Trump's list of must-win Senate races. The math is simple: Republicans need to get to a filibuster-proof, 60-seat Senate majority in order to ram through their radical right-wing agenda. And they can't get to 60 if they don't knock out Joe.

**That means that Joe Donnelly is standing in the way of the Trump/McConnell agenda. And that 's where you come in.**

**[Joe is facing a $35,000 fundraising deadline by the end of the month. Make a $5 grassroots contribution today to Joe's Senate Defense Fund, and your gift will be MATCHED.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c266e/7377f838/360c2842/51fb593b/1008278113/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jd-aprileom?refcode=20170428nd&amount=10")** | [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c266e/7377f838/360c2842/51fb593b/1008278113/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jd-aprileom?refcode=20170428nd&amount=10")
---
The attacks on Joe have already started -- coming from special interest groups like Trump's Make America Great PAC. **And they 're not going to stop until the polls close next November. We can't stop the attacks -- what we _can_ do is organize on the ground and fight back.**

Joe is ready for this fight. He's been attacked by Republicans and right-wing groups for a long time. They want to defeat Joe because he fights for our values. He's proven that by working to protect DACA recipients from deportation, standing against Trump's most extreme Cabinet nominees, and joining protesters to speak out against Trump's refugee ban and his disastrous attempt to take away people's health care.

What Joe's team needs are the resources to fight back -- online, on air, and on the ground. **We need him in Washington much more than Trump and McConnell want him gone.**

**[We can beat back the attacks that are already coming -- but only if we meet our fundraising goals. Make a $5 contribution today, and we'll match your gift.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c266e/7377f838/360c2842/51fb593b/1008278113/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jd-aprileom?refcode=20170428nd&amount=10")**

Thanks for your help,
Team Donnelly

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, APRIL 28th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
Did you know that April 23-29 is National Volunteer Week? President Nixon established National Volunteer Week with an executive order in 1974. He wanted to recognize and celebrate the efforts of volunteers. He understood, as do we here at the NHGOP, the value and importance of volunteers.
The time and talent that our State Committee members, City & County Chairs, regional Vice Chairs, grassroots activists and State Party officers give, help us be more effective in carrying out our vision and philosophy of limited government, personal responsibility and low taxes._**My sincere thanks to all of you**_ – most of you are also volunteering in your communities, serving on boards, and helping raise money for great causes.
A sincere thanks also to our General Court members, House and Senate, for all that you do in the name of good government!
Sincerely, _Jeanie_
_**[P.S. Don't miss your chance to hear from Kellyanne Conway, our special guest for our first fundraiser of the year. Be sure to reserve your seat for the May 18th event at the Nashua Radisso](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurva-2BR3ZynZFmQQsOtkDLtzhK6jhIpM9-2FPXc-2B7bPYHTWx_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2BRdjC-2B5Zvz2P94KVwOmkXG4-2FSjAOACuzNLndDaOF5XWx7xIbn8tRs3HR4UcCOjFRidsAZ-2Fr6qfLxUYhzgTzINxXpW8VRAEJq7uI91xWIAFBYrPRc2IXCvZUL8ege0L35bx6l2KQCgsmWqJdKszuMykEJ4mmg-2FwtdBR2cYRI8deEcN1yvS7LJ7-2FjKIKOl4nYwsgUemoNdtj8-2F20NwlxvCA6I4O8mb0f-2FKEWszK2ehyw-2FcYEPAvNhQeW4JL0-2BDZhc5Ofl15-2BeLYIFlBuKqlXb2Dg-3D-3D)**_[ _**n before it sells out!**_](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurva-2BR3ZynZFmQQsOtkDLtzhK6jhIpM9-2FPXc-2B7bPYHTWx_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2BRdjC-2B5Zvz2P94KVwOmkXG4-2FSjAOACuzNLndDaOF5XWx7xIbn8tRs3HR4UcCOjFRidsAZ-2Fr6qfLxUYhzgTzIN-2FQjsM4QP-2Bm3qBkH0rN2ZDEYpPeD0GO80kws02SuXoA7G4J2Vxqq4Ahv5xBJjeVaILgOyfaQNfF-2BxKlOEDlcI-2FM-2FMxrvB7xE6TSsm8m9mI67jo-2Fxr3bz3Ca7-2Fdy0ouJZ2zPvxwbhCtd509mX2v481DK-2BFHpsRidV1WBUdm9KKSrQI5Uswaw7Z3mr90bVnoMYtg-3D-3D)

| _**House Republicans**_
**House Committee Approves Strengthening Rape Shield Law** ****
On Tuesday, the House Judiciary committee passed SB 9, relative to the admissibility of proffered evidence in sexual assault cases, 11-4. The bill establishes a procedure to determine the admissibility of proffered evidence in sexual assault cases. This bill protects victims of sexual assault from having their sexual history brought in as evidence in a trial or appeals process.
Although this is in line with practices in New Hampshire, this bill simply clarifies the law. In a recent case, the court decided the Rape Shield Law didn’t apply during the appeals process. They later reversed their decision and called on the legislature to clarify the statute.
This legislation is an important protection for victims of sexual assault and their families, and this bill will allow for a victim’s sexual history to remain sealed from public release. SB9 will head to the full House for a vote on Thursday.
|

| _**Senate Republicans**_
**Innis applauds Senate vote to establish seacoast cancer cluster study commissions**
Concord, NH – Today, the New Hampshire Senate voted to establish two study commissions. HB 484 and HB 511 willcreate commissions to study the Seacoast cancer cluster and environmentally-triggered chronic illness.
Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle) issued the following statement:
“Environmentally-triggered chronic illness and specifically the Seacoast cancer cluster are troubling to families across New Hampshire” said Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle), a co-sponsor of the legislation. “This is one of the most pressing issues that I hear about from constituents on the Seacoast and I am grateful that the Senate worked in a bipartisan manner to pass this bill. I look forward to working with the commission to investigate the root cause of this problem and work to solve the issue.” |

---
| **Firecracker bill headed to Governor’s desk**
Concord, NH –The Senate concurred with the House of Representatives Thursday on SB23, a bill that would legalize firecrackers in New Hampshire. The bill now heads to the Governor’s desk.
Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua) issued the following statement:
“I am pleased the Senate and House could agree that in the live free or die state its citizens should be allowed to purchase firecrackers, a relatively low-grade form of fireworks,” said Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua). “By giving the people of New Hampshire the personal freedom to purchase and enjoy firecrackers, we are also giving small businesses the economic freedom to do more business in the state. I am proud to have been the prime sponsor of this legislation and look forward to this measure becoming law in the Granite State.” |

clean_body: | | | 8 NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, APRIL 28th | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, Did you know that April 23-29 is National Volunteer Week? President Nixon established National Volunteer Week with an executive order in 1974. He wanted to recognize and celebrate the efforts of volunteers. He understood, as do we here at the NHGOP, the value and importance of volunteers. The time and talent that our State Committee members, City & County Chairs, regional Vice Chairs, grassroots activists and State Party officers give, help us be more effective in carrying out our vision and philosophy of limited government, personal responsibility and low taxes.My sincere thanks to all of you – most of you are also volunteering in your communities, serving on boards, and helping raise money for great causes. A sincere thanks also to our General Court members, House and Senate, for all that you do in the name of good government! Sincerely, Jeanie | | | Join the Grassroots Circle! | | The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. | | | | Intern with the NH GOP! | | Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. | | | Upcoming Events | ---|---|--- | NHGOP Grassroots CommunicationsSummit - TOMORROW! April 29th - 8:30 AM St. Anselms College - Institute of Politics, 100 St. Anselm Drive, Manchester, NH For more information New Boston GOP Monthly Meeting May 4 - 7:00 PM Whipple Free Library, 67 Mount Vernon Road, New Boston, NH Prayers for Prevention - A grassroots movement to help prevent suicide & spread awareness May 7 Statewide For more information, NHGOP "Spring to Victory" Dinner with Special Guest Kellyanne Conway! May 18 - 6:00 PM Radisson Hotel, 11 Tara Blvd, Nashua, NH To RSVP & for more information | | | | | | Event Highlight | ---|---|--- | "Spring to Victory" Dinner with Special Guest Kellyanne Conway! | | We are hosting our first fundraising dinner of the year and we are so excited to have Kellyanne Conway as our special guest. We will also be awarding lots of great NH Republicans with the Norris Cotton awards, the "Rising Star" award, and the Meldrim Thomson "Principle over Politics" award. Your participation in this event will be helping the Party continue to grow our campaign operation as we head into 2018. Starting now will ensure our Republican candidate save the resources they need to win and protect our hard-fought victories in 2016. This dinner will be sold-out soon, so don't wait to purchase until the last minute! | ---|---|--- | | Photo of the Week | ---|---|--- Governor Chris Sununu and Governor Craig Benson at Thursday's unveiling of Governor Benson's portrait at the State House! | Republican Spotlight! | ---|---|--- | Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight shines on Joe Alexander, Jr! He was nominated by the newly elected NH College Republican Chairman, Dan Passen. Joe was the CR Chairman in 2016 and has grown the College Republicans exponentially during his tenure. He was heavily involved in Kelly Ayotte's U.S. Senate campaign and also ran for State Representative in Concord. We appreciate all of Joe's hard work for the College Republicans and for the NHGOP. He is graduating from St. Anselm College this May and has committed to continue helping New Hampshire Republicans win! Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans House Committee Approves Strengthening Rape Shield Law ** On Tuesday, the House Judiciary committee passed SB 9, relative to the admissibility of proffered evidence in sexual assault cases, 11-4. The bill establishes a procedure to determine the admissibility of proffered evidence in sexual assault cases. This bill protects victims of sexual assault from having their sexual history brought in as evidence in a trial or appeals process. Although this is in line with practices in New Hampshire, this bill simply clarifies the law. In a recent case, the court decided the Rape Shield Law didn’t apply during the appeals process. They later reversed their decision and called on the legislature to clarify the statute. This legislation is an important protection for victims of sexual assault and their families, and this bill will allow for a victim’s sexual history to remain sealed from public release. SB9 will head to the full House for a vote on Thursday. | | Senate Republicans Innis applauds Senate vote to establish seacoast cancer cluster study commissions Concord, NH – Today, the New Hampshire Senate voted to establish two study commissions. HB 484 and HB 511 willcreate commissions to study the Seacoast cancer cluster and environmentally-triggered chronic illness. Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle) issued the following statement: “Environmentally-triggered chronic illness and specifically the Seacoast cancer cluster are troubling to families across New Hampshire” said Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle), a co-sponsor of the legislation. “This is one of the most pressing issues that I hear about from constituents on the Seacoast and I am grateful that the Senate worked in a bipartisan manner to pass this bill. I look forward to working with the commission to investigate the root cause of this problem and work to solve the issue.” | | Firecracker bill headed to Governor’s desk Concord, NH –The Senate concurred with the House of Representatives Thursday on SB23, a bill that would legalize firecrackers in New Hampshire. The bill now heads to the Governor’s desk. Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua) issued the following statement: “I am pleased the Senate and House could agree that in the live free or die state its citizens should be allowed to purchase firecrackers, a relatively low-grade form of fireworks,” said Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua). “By giving the people of New Hampshire the personal freedom to purchase and enjoy firecrackers, we are also giving small businesses the economic freedom to do more business in the state. I am proud to have been the prime sponsor of this legislation and look forward to this measure becoming law in the Granite State.” | | President Trump's First 100 Days! | ---|---|--- | Click below to flip through some of President Trump's most notable days since taking office! | | This Week's Best Tweets! | ---|---|--- | Tweet With Us! | | ---|---|--- | Connect with your Republican Leaders! | ---|---|--- | | | | | Speaker of the House Shawn Jasper | | | Senate President Chuck Morse | | | Executive Council | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | | | Governor Chris Sununu | | | Chairman Forrester & The NH GOP | ---|---|--- | | Web - "LIKE" on Facebook \- "FOLLOW" on Twitter - | | | Web - Facebook \- Twitter - Chairman Jeanie Forrester "LIKE" on Facebook - "FOLLOW" on Twitter - "FOLLOW" The Staff on Twitter Finance Director - Political Director -** | | 10 Water Street, Concord, NH 03301 Phone: (603) 225-9341 | Email: info@nhgop.org | | | ---|---|--- | | mailto:? | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire · 10 Water Street · Concord · New Hampshire · 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBE_ALT--

body:
Peter,
We've had a busy week fighting for you!

On Monday, we signed important ride-sharing legislation that permits the use of services like Uber and Lyft. These ride-sharing companies make our communities safer and will create thousands of new jobs here in Missouri. Check out [this short video](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=8195ac0f1d&e=84041254e6):
[](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=c161414024&e=84041254e6)
Then on Tuesday, Fox & Friends wanted to hear about our conservative agenda and how we are supporting law enforcement. See the interview [here](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=4f2c5038b9&e=84041254e6).
[](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=cf9d2ccf7a&e=84041254e6)
It's an honor to be getting results for you. Thanks for your prayers and continued support as we fight for more jobs and higher pay.

Yours in service,
Eric Greitens
Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on [Facebook](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=b9b1f44f44&e=84041254e6) and [Twitter](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=574cc4165f&e=84041254e6). Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected. If they become too much, you can [unsubscribe](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=06c00a84b5).
